question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/transaction.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: constructTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user cause constructTransaction() to produce an incorrect transactionType label for all operations by exploiting the fact that types.GetTransactionType(int32(firstTransaction.Type)) is called with an int16-to-int32 conversion, and if firstTransaction.Type is a negative int16 value, the int32 conversion produces a large positive number that maps to an unknown/wrong transaction type, misrepresenting Hashgraph history?
